label: Road crash costs by severity (national and state)

fatal.label: Cost per fatal crash
fatal.note: Includes VSL, property damage, emergency services, hospital, legal, insurance. Higher than VPF alone because it includes all crash costs.

state_variations.description: State road agencies publish their own crash cost estimates. Values vary by 15-30% due to different methodologies and local cost structures.

nsw.source: Transport for NSW Economic Parameter Values

victoria.source: VicRoads / Department of Transport and Planning

queensland.source: Department of Transport and Main Roads

state_variations.note: Use state-specific values when available. National averages for cross-state or national-level analysis.

source.publication: ATAP PV2: Road Parameter Values; BITRE Road Crash Costs in Australia
source.publisher: Austroads; Bureau of Infrastructure and Transport Research Economics
source.update_frequency: periodic (BITRE major update every 5-7 years, state values updated more frequently)

caveats: Crash costs include all components (human, property, emergency, legal, insurance). Not directly comparable with UK casualty-only costs.
State values differ due to methodology, not just cost differences. Compare with caution.
Under-reporting of minor crashes means the minor injury and PDO values may underestimate true costs.
